pages.full_text:
We drove along winding roads through beautiful little villages, & arrived at Etraques, where it's market day, to shop. Then on up the valley of the Truyères, with hydro electric dams, & purpose-built 1930's villages.

[Route sketch of France, labelled 1992, with route line showing:] le thore / Vallée de la Seine / Châteaux de la Loire / Bourges / Périgord Caves / Gorges de Truyères / Cassaniouze / Conques

We had lunch in a very dirty wood & came on to a crowded, but pleasant campsite on the La Source* * * Lac du Sarrans, created by the Sarrans
pages.route_sketch_description: Outline map of France with a route line drawn from le thore / Vallée de la Seine in the north, down through Châteaux de la Loire and Bourges, then south-west to Périgord Caves, and south to Gorges de Truyères, Cassaniouze, and Conques. Year label '1992' appears to the right of the map.
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread — first word is mid-sentence. 'Etraques' may be read as 'Estaques' or similar; written clearly but could be a variant spelling. 'Truyères' confirmed by match with right-page reference to the Gorges. 'La Source***' appears to be a campsite name with a star rating. Text at bottom is cut off at page edge ('Sarrans' continues onto right page).

pages.full_text:
damn. There's a swimming pool where we all enjoyed swimming, & John grew more & more confident swimming out from the chute. We all had an ice cream afterwards to celebrate his swimming the width.

[Sketch of pool layout at campsite, labelled:] sun-warmed seat / bridge / foot bath / chute / deep / swimming / shoe / 4 sprays into pool / steps down / chil's pool+slide

Pool in confined area at campsite.     eating

Wash-rooms under tennis court.

__Wed August 5th__

      We wanted to leave early, but stayed for a swim. The pool only opened at 10.00.. We drove north [past] out of the Gorges & the Chestnut Trees. We spent hours on the A71, going past villages, castles & farm houses. We drove for 8 hours & ended up camping in quite a pretty campsite in Salbris * * *. The restaurant,
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn diagram of the swimming pool layout at the campsite, showing: chute on the left leading into the pool, 'deep' and 'swimming' sections, a bridge above, sun-warmed seat at top left, foot bath at top right, a 'shoe' area, '4 sprays into pool' entering from the right, 'steps down', and a 'chil's pool+slide' at bottom left. Also annotated 'eating' area outside the pool. Caption reads 'Pool in confined area at campsite.'
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence ('damn.' — continuation of 'Sarrans dam' from left page). The word struck through before 'out' appears to be 'past' — crossed out and not replaced. 'Salbris***' is a campsite with star rating. 'chil's pool+slide' likely abbreviation for 'children's pool+slide'. Text is cut off at bottom — diary entry continues onto next spread. The date 'Wed August 5th' with 1992 context: August 5 1992 was indeed a Wednesday, supporting confidence.
